model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the button in the top-left corner
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (308,151) said dx=-455 dy=-470 conf=0.80 -> (-147,-319)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:

The current crosshair position is at (-147, -319) based on the previous iteration's result.

Looking at the image, the crosshair (red circle with cross) is in the top-left corner.
The target is the green button in the top-left.
The crosshair is at approximately (0, 0) relative to the screen origin (if the screen is 1000x1000, the image shows it very close to the top-left).
Looking at the image, the crosshair is slightly to the left and above the green button.
Green button center is roughly at (45, 30).
Crosshair is at roughly (0, 0).
Actually, let's look closer.
The crosshair is at (0, 0) in coordinates? No, the image is the current screen state.
The crosshair is at the very top left corner of the screen.
The green button is just to the right and slightly below the crosshair.
Center of green button: x ~ 45, y ~ 30.
Crosshair position: x ~ 0, y ~ 0.
Offset: dx = 45 - 0 = 45, dy = 30 - 0 = 30.
Let's re-evaluate the crosshair position. It seems to be exactly at (0,0).
Green button: x starts at 10, ends at 80 (width ~ 70). Center x ~ 45.
y starts at 10, ends at 60 (height ~ 50). Center y ~ 35.
Offset dx = 45, dy = 35.
Confidence is high.
